amendments.body: Issue 3 is still not implemented. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:3042-3060`, `record_task_run_start` intentionally leaves `TaskRunEntry.plan_hash` unset for new rows and only records `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation`; in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2583-2621`, `safe_plan_hash_backfill` refuses to fill `plan_hash` when provenance is `None`, so legacy/open rows with `plan_hash: None` are never backfilled from the milestone snapshot. The new tests at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4727-4816` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5392-5506` explicitly assert that non-backfilling behavior, so the suite now locks in semantics that contradict the task prompt’s required adapter-level snapshot backfill for both new rows and existing open rows.
amendments.rationale: This leaves lineage rows missing the canonical plan hash whenever callers omit it, so the accepted amendment and acceptance criterion for automatic snapshot backfill are unmet.

payload.summary: Reviewed the milestone-lineage changes in `src/adapters/fs.rs`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`, and the touched tests, and ran `cargo test milestone_record --lib`. Journal payload reuse and runless-to-named completion look correct, but the plan-hash backfill change was intentionally implemented with different semantics than the task requires.
